summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn welcomed Phoenix Suns player Cameron Johnson for a special interview, exploring his journey of faith, family, and basketball. Cameron shared how his path to the NBA was marked by setbacks, including a playoff loss in high school and multiple surgeries, which his mother framed as part of God's plan. He emphasized that his career is a result of divine blessings and the influence of godly people, including his parents, coaches like Monty Williams, and teammates. Cameron highlighted the importance of a supportive Christian community, from college Bible studies to pre-game chapel with the Suns, in strengthening his walk with God. His favorite scripture, Psalm 23, guides him daily, reminding him of God's provision, guidance, and comfort. Pastor Travis concluded by reflecting on the themes of Psalm 23, encouraging the congregation to trust God in valleys of fear and to experience His overflowing favor. The service ended with an invitation to surrender to Jesus, emphasizing that a relationship with Christ is the ultimate purpose.
mainPoints: God's plan often unfolds through setbacks and unexpected turns, requiring faith and trust in His timing.
A supportive community of believers is crucial for spiritual growth and perseverance in one's journey.
Scripture, like Psalm 23, provides daily comfort, guidance, and a reminder of God's provision and favor.

versesMentioned.text: The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing. He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters, he refreshes my soul. He guides me along the right paths for his name’s sake. Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me. You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies. You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows. Surely your goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the Lord forever.
And my God will meet all your needs according to the riches of his glory in Christ Jesus.
So Abraham called that place The Lord Will Provide. And to this day it is said, 'On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.'

Monday.scripture: Psalm 23:1 - 'The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing.'
Monday.action: Identify one worry or need today and consciously choose to trust God as your provider.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for being my shepherd. Help me to trust that you will supply all my needs today.

Tuesday.scripture: Psalm 23:2 - 'He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters.'
Tuesday.action: Intentionally schedule 10 minutes of quiet time to rest and reflect, away from distractions.
Tuesday.prayer: Shepherd, lead me to your quiet waters today. Restore my soul and calm my spirit.

Wednesday.scripture: Psalm 23:3 - 'He guides me along the right paths for his name’s sake.'
Wednesday.action: Before making a decision today, pause to ask God for wisdom and direction.
Wednesday.prayer: God, guide my steps today. Help me to walk in the path of righteousness for your name's sake.

Thursday.scripture: Psalm 23:4 - 'Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me.'
Thursday.action: Write down one fear or anxiety and speak aloud: 'God is with me in this.'
Thursday.prayer: Father, when I am afraid, remind me that you are with me. Your presence comforts me.

Friday.scripture: Psalm 23:5 - 'You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows.'
Friday.action: Do one act of generosity for someone else, reflecting God's overflowing blessing.
Friday.prayer: Lord, thank you for anointing me with your Spirit. Let your abundance flow through me to others.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 23:6 - 'Surely your goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on one way you've experienced God's goodness this week and share it with a friend or family member.
Saturday.prayer: God, I thank you for your goodness and mercy that chase after me. I trust in your faithful love forever.
